dialogueChunks.intro: Our stores are empty. The children stopped asking for second helpings weeks ago because they know there aren't any.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: A trader at {quest_origin} has grain to spare, but the road passes through {enemy_type} territory. Three volunteers have offered to drive the caravan knowing full well the odds. One of them is the baker's mother. She said she'd rather die on the road than watch her grandchildren waste away.
dialogueChunks.outro: They leave at dawn to {quest_action}. {quest_threat} will be waiting somewhere along the route. Those volunteers aren't fighters: they're parents and grandparents who decided {quest_stakes} matter more than their own safety.

playerResponses.text: I'll ride with them. They won't face the road alone.
Can we find a safer route instead?
I have my own supplies to worry about.

dialogueChunks.intro: The river has been rising for three days. {quest_focus} won't hold much longer, and everything downstream: homes, farms, families, is in the path.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The mason volunteered to go into the water and shore up the weakest section with his own hands. He knows that if the dam breaks while he's down there, he's gone. His wife begged him not to go. He kissed her forehead and said 'Those are our neighbors' children down the hill.'
dialogueChunks.outro: He can't do it alone. We need someone to {quest_action} while he works the foundation. {quest_stakes} depend on whether that wall holds through tonight.

playerResponses.text: Let's get to the dam. Every minute counts.
Wouldn't it be smarter to evacuate?
I'm not going near a collapsing dam.

bindings.quest_time_pressure: before the war band closes in

dialogueChunks.intro: Sixty people are on the road right now, walking with everything they own on their backs. Behind them, the {enemy_type} are burning what's left.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The village elder stayed behind to buy time. She barricaded the gates and lit fires to make the settlement look occupied. She's seventy years old and she's pretending to be a garrison. {quest_focus} has maybe half a day's lead, and the road ahead isn't clear.
dialogueChunks.outro: We need someone to {quest_action} so {quest_stakes} can reach safety. {quest_time_pressure}. That old woman is giving everything she has left so her people can walk a little further.

playerResponses.text: I'll make sure her sacrifice means something.
Can we go back for the elder too?
